nodes.label: LANDMARC RFID Indoor Localization System
nodes.source_file: raw/Sci-Hub. An Indoor Localization Mechanism Using Active RFID Tag : IEEE International Conference on S.pdf
nodes.rationale: LANDMARC introduces reference tags to reduce the number of deployed RFID readers while maintaining localization accuracy; this trade-off lowers deployment cost.

nodes.label: RFID Reference Tag Concept
nodes.source_file: raw/Sci-Hub. An Indoor Localization Mechanism Using Active RFID Tag : IEEE International Conference on S.pdf
nodes.rationale: Reference tags serve as known anchor points reducing expensive RFID reader deployment while enabling triangulation-based localization.

nodes.label: Analysis of WLAN RSSI for Indoor Location Fingerprinting (Kaemarungsi & Krishnamurthy, 2012)
nodes.source_file: raw/Sci-Hub. Analysis of WLAN's received signal strength indication for indoor location fingerprinting .pdf
nodes.author: Kamol Kaemarungsi, Prashant Krishnamurthy
nodes.norm_label: analysis of wlan rssi for indoor location fingerprinting (kaemarungsi & krishnamurthy, 2012)

nodes.label: WLAN RSSI Statistical Characteristics for Fingerprinting
nodes.source_file: raw/Sci-Hub. Analysis of WLAN's received signal strength indication for indoor location fingerprinting .pdf
nodes.rationale: Thorough statistical analysis of 802.11b/g RSSI properties helps designers accurately model positioning systems and improve fingerprinting accuracy.
nodes.norm_label: wlan rssi statistical characteristics for fingerprinting

nodes.label: Bluetooth Indoor Localization with Multiple Neural Networks (Altini et al., ISWPC 2010)
nodes.source_file: raw/Sci-Hub. Bluetooth indoor localization with multiple neural networks : IEEE 5th International Sympos.pdf
nodes.source_location: IEEE 5th International Symposium on Wireless Pervasive Computing 2010
nodes.author: Marco Altini, Davide Brunelli, Elisabetta Farella, Luca Benini
nodes.norm_label: bluetooth indoor localization with multiple neural networks (altini et al., iswpc 2010)

nodes.label: Multiple Neural Networks Architecture for Bluetooth Localization
nodes.source_file: raw/Sci-Hub. Bluetooth indoor localization with multiple neural networks : IEEE 5th International Sympos.pdf
nodes.rationale: Multiple neural networks tailored per user orientation handle RSSI variability caused by the user's body absorbing RF signals; separate NNs handle node failures, improving resilience.
nodes.norm_label: multiple neural networks architecture for bluetooth localization

nodes.label: User Orientation Effect on RSSI for Bluetooth Localization
nodes.source_file: raw/Sci-Hub. Bluetooth indoor localization with multiple neural networks : IEEE 5th International Sympos.pdf
nodes.rationale: Body absorption of RF signal differs with user orientation, causing systematic RSSI shifts that degrade single-model localization; orientation-specific NNs mitigate this.
nodes.norm_label: user orientation effect on rssi for bluetooth localization

nodes.label: CSI-Based Indoor Localization - FILA System (IEEE TPDS 2013)
nodes.source_file: raw/Sci-Hub. CSI-Based Indoor Localization : IEEE Transactions on Parallel and Distributed Systems, 2013.pdf
nodes.source_location: IEEE Transactions on Parallel and Distributed Systems, Vol. 24, No. 7, July 2013
nodes.norm_label: csi-based indoor localization - fila system (ieee tpds 2013)

nodes.label: FILA - Fine-grained Indoor Localization via CSI
nodes.source_file: raw/Sci-Hub. CSI-Based Indoor Localization : IEEE Transactions on Parallel and Distributed Systems, 2013.pdf
nodes.rationale: FILA uses PHY-layer CSI from OFDM 802.11 NICs (30 subcarriers) for both propagation model and fingerprinting, achieving finer spatial resolution than RSSI-only approaches by exploiting frequency and spatial diversity.
nodes.norm_label: fila - fine-grained indoor localization via csi

nodes.label: OFDM Subcarrier Frequency Diversity for Indoor Localization
nodes.source_file: raw/Sci-Hub. CSI-Based Indoor Localization : IEEE Transactions on Parallel and Distributed Systems, 2013.pdf
nodes.rationale: Using CSI across 30 OFDM subcarriers provides frequency diversity that mitigates multipath fading effects dominating single-frequency RSSI measurements.
nodes.norm_label: ofdm subcarrier frequency diversity for indoor localization

nodes.label: Design and Analysis of a Bluetooth-Based Indoor Localization System - BIPS (Bruno & Delmastro, 2003)
nodes.source_file: raw/Sci-Hub. Design and Analysis of a Bluetooth-Based Indoor Localization System : Lecture Notes in Comp.pdf
nodes.source_location: Lecture Notes in Computer Science 2775, 2003, pp. 711-725
nodes.author: Raffaele Bruno, Franca Delmastro
nodes.norm_label: design and analysis of a bluetooth-based indoor localization system - bips (bruno & delmastro, 2003)

nodes.label: BIPS - Bluetooth Indoor Positioning System
nodes.source_file: raw/Sci-Hub. Design and Analysis of a Bluetooth-Based Indoor Localization System : Lecture Notes in Comp.pdf
nodes.rationale: BIPS integrates Bluetooth device discovery (inquiry procedures) into existing Bluetooth access point network infrastructure to provide cost-effective positioning without dedicated sensors.

nodes.label: Evaluation of the Reliability of RSSI for Indoor Localization (Dong & Dargie, 2012)
nodes.source_file: raw/Sci-Hub. Evaluation of the reliability of RSSI for indoor localization : 2012 International Conferen.pdf
nodes.source_location: 2012 International Conference on Wireless Communications in Underground and Confined Areas
nodes.author: Qian Dong, Waltenegus Dargie
nodes.norm_label: evaluation of the reliability of rssi for indoor localization (dong & dargie, 2012)

nodes.label: RSSI Unreliability Finding for Indoor Localization
nodes.source_file: raw/Sci-Hub. Evaluation of the reliability of RSSI for indoor localization : 2012 International Conferen.pdf
nodes.rationale: Experimental calibration mapping RSSI to distance shows unacceptably high errors in indoor WSN environments, motivating use of CSI or fingerprinting rather than pure range estimation from RSSI.
nodes.norm_label: rssi unreliability finding for indoor localization

nodes.label: Indoor Localization Improvement via Adaptive RSS Fingerprinting Database (Koweerawong et al., ICOIN 2013)
nodes.source_file: raw/Sci-Hub. Indoor localization improvement via adaptive RSS fingerprinting database : The Internationa.pdf
nodes.author: Chavalit Koweerawong, Komwut Wipusitwarakun, Kamol Kaemarungsi
nodes.norm_label: indoor localization improvement via adaptive rss fingerprinting database (koweerawong et al., icoin 2013)

nodes.label: Feedback-Based Adaptive RSS Database with Spatial Interpolation
nodes.source_file: raw/Sci-Hub. Indoor localization improvement via adaptive RSS fingerprinting database : The Internationa.pdf
nodes.rationale: Using a small number of re-measured 'feedback' points and spatial interpolation propagates database updates to adjacent locations, reducing database staleness without full re-survey costs.
nodes.norm_label: feedback-based adaptive rss database with spatial interpolation

nodes.label: Indoor Localization Without the Pain - EZ System (Chintalapudi et al., MobiCom 2010)
nodes.source_file: raw/Sci-Hub. Indoor localization without the pain : Proceedings of the sixteenth annual international co.pdf
nodes.author: Krishna Chintalapudi, Anand Padmanabha Iyer, Venkata N. Padmanabhan
nodes.norm_label: indoor localization without the pain - ez system (chintalapudi et al., mobicom 2010)

nodes.label: EZ - Zero Pre-Deployment Indoor Localization Algorithm
nodes.source_file: raw/Sci-Hub. Indoor localization without the pain : Proceedings of the sixteenth annual international co.pdf
nodes.rationale: EZ uses physics constraints of wireless propagation plus a genetic algorithm to solve simultaneous RF environment learning and user localization, requiring only occasional GPS fixes at building entrances - eliminating manual site surveys.
nodes.norm_label: ez - zero pre-deployment indoor localization algorithm

nodes.label: Optimal Placement of Reference Nodes for Wireless Indoor Positioning (Aomumpai et al., ECTI-CON 2014)
nodes.source_file: raw/Sci-Hub. Optimal placement of reference nodes for wireless indoor positioning systems : 2014 11th In.pdf
nodes.author: Supattra Aomumpai, Kittipob Kondee, Chutima Prommak, Kamol Kaemarungsi
nodes.norm_label: optimal placement of reference nodes for wireless indoor positioning (aomumpai et al., ecti-con 2014)

nodes.label: Binary Integer Linear Programming for Reference Node Placement
nodes.source_file: raw/Sci-Hub. Optimal placement of reference nodes for wireless indoor positioning systems : 2014 11th In.pdf
nodes.rationale: Formulating reference node placement as BILP minimizes the number of nodes while maximizing coverage, yielding up to 5 meters accuracy improvement at 90th percentile over ad-hoc deployments.
nodes.norm_label: binary integer linear programming for reference node placement

nodes.label: RSSI-Based Indoor Localization With IoT (Sadowski & Spachos, IEEE Access 2018)
nodes.source_file: raw/Sci-Hub. RSSI-Based Indoor Localization With the Internet of Things : IEEE Access, 2018.pdf
nodes.author: Sebastian Sadowski, Petros Spachos
nodes.norm_label: rssi-based indoor localization with iot (sadowski & spachos, ieee access 2018)

nodes.label: Comparative IoT Technology Evaluation for Indoor Localization (WiFi/BLE/ZigBee/LoRaWAN)
nodes.source_file: raw/Sci-Hub. RSSI-Based Indoor Localization With the Internet of Things : IEEE Access, 2018.pdf
nodes.rationale: Comparing WiFi, BLE, ZigBee and LoRaWAN on RSSI trilateration under same conditions provides empirical guidance for technology selection based on accuracy vs power consumption trade-offs.
nodes.norm_label: comparative iot technology evaluation for indoor localization (wifi/ble/zigbee/lorawan)

nodes.label: Recent Advances in Indoor Localization: A Survey on Theoretical Approaches and Applications (Yassin et al., IEEE COMST 2017)
nodes.source_file: raw/Sci-Hub. Recent Advances in Indoor Localization: A Survey on Theoretical Approaches and Applications.pdf
nodes.author: Ali Yassin, Youssef Nasser, Mariette Awad, Ahmed Al-Dubai, Ran Liu, Chau Yuen, Ronald Raulefs, Elias Aboutanios
nodes.norm_label: recent advances in indoor localization: a survey on theoretical approaches and applications (yassin et al., ieee comst 2017)

nodes.label: Hybrid Data Fusion for Indoor Localization
nodes.source_file: raw/Sci-Hub. Recent Advances in Indoor Localization: A Survey on Theoretical Approaches and Applications.pdf
nodes.rationale: Combining heterogeneous radio access technologies (e.g., cellular + WLAN) using data fusion addresses hearability limitations and improves coverage and accuracy beyond single-technology approaches.
nodes.norm_label: hybrid data fusion for indoor localization

nodes.label: The Microsoft Indoor Localization Competition: Experiences and Lessons Learned (Lymberopoulos & Liu, IEEE SPM 2017)
nodes.source_file: raw/Sci-Hub. The Microsoft Indoor Localization Competition: Experiences and Lessons Learned : IEEE Signa.pdf
nodes.author: Dimitrios Lymberopoulos, Jie Liu
nodes.norm_label: the microsoft indoor localization competition: experiences and lessons learned (lymberopoulos & liu, ieee spm 2017)

nodes.label: Microsoft Indoor Localization Competition Benchmark (2014-2017)
nodes.source_file: raw/Sci-Hub. The Microsoft Indoor Localization Competition: Experiences and Lessons Learned : IEEE Signa.pdf
nodes.rationale: Standardized competition environment across 4 years (100+ teams) enables direct comparison of heterogeneous indoor localization approaches in realistic, unfamiliar settings, addressing the lack of common evaluation benchmarks in the field.

nodes.label: WiDeep: WiFi-based Accurate and Robust Indoor Localization System using Deep Learning (Abbas et al., PerCom 2019)
nodes.source_file: raw/Sci-Hub. WiDeep: WiFi-based Accurate and Robust Indoor Localization System using Deep Learning : 201.pdf
nodes.author: Moustafa Abbas, Moustafa Elhamshary, Hamada Rizk, Marwan Torki, Moustafa Youssef
nodes.norm_label: wideep: wifi-based accurate and robust indoor localization system using deep learning (abbas et al., percom 2019)

nodes.label: Stacked Denoising Autoencoders for WiFi Fingerprint Localization
nodes.source_file: raw/Sci-Hub. WiDeep: WiFi-based Accurate and Robust Indoor Localization System using Deep Learning : 201.pdf
nodes.rationale: Stacked denoising autoencoders combined with a probabilistic fusion framework learn noise-robust latent representations of WiFi fingerprints, outperforming conventional approaches on heterogeneous devices.
nodes.norm_label: stacked denoising autoencoders for wifi fingerprint localization

nodes.label: Survey of Indoor Localization Based on Deep Learning (Kordi et al., CMC 2024)
nodes.author: Khaldon Azzam Kordi, Mardeni Roslee, Mohamad Yusoff Alias, Abdulraqeb Alhammadi, Athar Waseem, Anwar Faizd Osman
nodes.norm_label: survey of indoor localization based on deep learning (kordi et al., cmc 2024)

nodes.label: Hybrid Deep Learning Approach for Multi-Modal Indoor Localization
nodes.rationale: Combining CNN and RNN architectures with multi-sensor inputs (WiFi, BLE, UWB, ZigBee) exploits complementary signal properties and temporal dynamics to improve localization robustness in obstructed environments.
nodes.norm_label: hybrid deep learning approach for multi-modal indoor localization

nodes.label: Device-Free Indoor Localization via Deep Learning
nodes.rationale: Device-free localization detects targets via their effect on existing wireless signals without requiring the target to carry hardware; deep learning extracts features from CSI perturbations for this purpose.
nodes.norm_label: device-free indoor localization via deep learning

nodes.label: Efficient Kernel Based 3-D Source Localization via Tensor Completion (Lu et al., KSII TIIS 2019)
nodes.source_location: KSII Transactions on Internet and Information Systems, Vol. 13, No. 1, Jan 2019
nodes.author: Shan Lu, Jun Zhang, Xianmin Ma, Changju Kan
nodes.norm_label: efficient kernel based 3-d source localization via tensor completion (lu et al., ksii tiis 2019)

nodes.label: Tensor Completion for Incomplete RSS Fingerprint Database Recovery
nodes.rationale: Exploiting the low-rank property of RSS spatial correlation matrices allows tensor completion to recover missing fingerprint entries from sparse measurements, drastically reducing site-survey overhead for 3D environments.
nodes.norm_label: tensor completion for incomplete rss fingerprint database recovery

nodes.label: Kernel-Based Learning for Fingerprint Matching
nodes.rationale: Kernel-based matching in the positioning phase clarifies the non-linear relationship between fingerprint measurements and locations, improving sensitivity and accuracy beyond nearest-neighbor approaches.

nodes.label: An Efficient Fingerprint Database Construction Approach Based on Matrix Completion (Tan et al., IEEE Access 2020)
nodes.source_file: raw/Tan et al. - 2020 - An Efficient Fingerprint Database Construction App.pdf | My Library | Zotero.pdf
nodes.author: Teng Tan, Lingwen Zhang, Qiumei Li
nodes.norm_label: an efficient fingerprint database construction approach based on matrix completion (tan et al., ieee access 2020)

nodes.label: Weighted Nuclear Norm Matrix Completion for RSS Fingerprint Recovery
nodes.source_file: raw/Tan et al. - 2020 - An Efficient Fingerprint Database Construction App.pdf | My Library | Zotero.pdf
nodes.rationale: Converting matrix completion to weighted nuclear norm with L1 and Frobenius norms simultaneously reduces outlier influence and Gaussian noise, improving recovery fidelity over standard nuclear norm minimization.
nodes.norm_label: weighted nuclear norm matrix completion for rss fingerprint recovery

nodes.label: Through-the-Wall Detection of Stationary Human Targets Using Doppler Radar (Narayanan et al., PIER-B 2010)
nodes.source_location: Progress In Electromagnetics Research B, Vol. 20, 147-166, 2010
nodes.author: R. M. Narayanan, M. C. Shastry, P.-H. Chen, M. Levi
nodes.norm_label: through-the-wall detection of stationary human targets using doppler radar (narayanan et al., pier-b 2010)

nodes.label: Doppler Radar Through-Wall Human Detection
nodes.rationale: Detecting Doppler signatures from breathing and limb micro-movements allows identification of stationary humans behind walls using a 750 MHz Doppler radar; EMD algorithm extracts transient non-stationary features.

nodes.label: Empirical Mode Decomposition (EMD) for Doppler Feature Extraction
nodes.rationale: EMD adaptively decomposes non-stationary Doppler signals into intrinsic mode functions without requiring a priori basis, enabling extraction of irregular human motion signatures.
nodes.norm_label: empirical mode decomposition (emd) for doppler feature extraction

nodes.label: Miskolc IIS Hybrid IPS Dataset for Hybrid Indoor Positioning (Toth & Tamas, Radioelektronika 2016)
nodes.source_file: raw/Toth and Tamas - 2016 - Miskolc IIS hybrid IPS Dataset for hybrid indoor .pdf | My Library | Zotero.pdf
nodes.source_location: 26th Conference Radioelektronika 2016, April 19-20, Kosice, Slovak Republic
nodes.author: Zsolt Toth, Judit Tamas
nodes.norm_label: miskolc iis hybrid ips dataset for hybrid indoor positioning (toth & tamas, radioelektronika 2016)

nodes.label: Miskolc IIS Hybrid Indoor Positioning Dataset (WiFi/BT/Magnetometer)
nodes.source_file: raw/Toth and Tamas - 2016 - Miskolc IIS hybrid IPS Dataset for hybrid indoor .pdf | My Library | Zotero.pdf
nodes.rationale: Multi-technology dataset (WLAN, Bluetooth, Magnetometer) in a 3-story reinforced concrete building enables benchmark comparison of hybrid indoor positioning algorithms that cannot be evaluated with single-modality datasets.
nodes.norm_label: miskolc iis hybrid indoor positioning dataset (wifi/bt/magnetometer)

nodes.label: UWB Indoor Localization Using Deep Learning LSTM Networks (Poulose & Han, Applied Sciences 2020)
nodes.author: Alwin Poulose, Dong Seog Han
nodes.norm_label: uwb indoor localization using deep learning lstm networks (poulose & han, applied sciences 2020)

nodes.label: Two-Layer LSTM Architecture for UWB TOA-Distance Localization
nodes.rationale: Two-layer LSTM uses TOA-distance inputs from UWB anchors; feedback loops in LSTM serve as memory to handle time-series position sequence, achieving 7cm mean error by mitigating multipath and NLOS errors without explicit trilateration.
nodes.norm_label: two-layer lstm architecture for uwb toa-distance localization

nodes.label: Fast Construction of the Radio Map Based on Improved Low-Rank Matrix Completion (Wang et al., Journal of Sensors 2021)
nodes.source_file: raw/Wang et al. - 2021 - Fast Construction of the Radio Map Based on the Im.pdf | My Library | Zotero.pdf
nodes.source_location: Journal of Sensors, Volume 2021, Article ID 2017208
nodes.author: Zhuang Wang, Liye Zhang, Qun Kong, Kangtao Wang
nodes.norm_label: fast construction of the radio map based on improved low-rank matrix completion (wang et al., journal of sensors 2021)

nodes.label: Frobenius Parameter for Stabilized Low-Rank Matrix Completion in Radio Maps
nodes.source_file: raw/Wang et al. - 2021 - Fast Construction of the Radio Map Based on the Im.pdf | My Library | Zotero.pdf
nodes.rationale: Adding a Frobenius norm parameter to the traditional low-rank model controls solution instability during sparse-to-dense radio map filling; a separate recovery step eliminates environmental noise.
nodes.norm_label: frobenius parameter for stabilized low-rank matrix completion in radio maps

nodes.label: A Survey on Wireless Indoor Localization from the Device Perspective (Xiao et al., ACM CSUR 2016)
nodes.source_file: raw/Xiao et al. - 2016 - A Survey on Wireless Indoor Localization from the .pdf | My Library | Zotero.pdf
nodes.source_location: ACM Computing Surveys, Vol. 49, No. 2, Article 25, June 2016
nodes.author: Jiang Xiao, Zimu Zhou, Youwen Yi, Lionel M. Ni
nodes.norm_label: a survey on wireless indoor localization from the device perspective (xiao et al., acm csur 2016)

nodes.label: Device-Based vs Device-Free Wireless Indoor Localization Taxonomy
nodes.source_file: raw/Xiao et al. - 2016 - A Survey on Wireless Indoor Localization from the .pdf | My Library | Zotero.pdf
nodes.rationale: Classifying localization into device-based (target carries wireless device) and device-free (infrastructure detects impact on signals) clarifies the design space and trade-offs in terms of accuracy, cost, scalability and energy efficiency.
nodes.norm_label: device-based vs device-free wireless indoor localization taxonomy

nodes.label: Zero-Configuration Robust Indoor Localization - SVD-based System (Lim et al., UIUC TR 2005)
nodes.source_file: raw/Zero-Configuration, Robust Indoor Localization Theory and Experimentation.pdf
nodes.source_location: Technical Report UIUCDCS-R-2005-2629, University of Illinois at Urbana-Champaign, August 2005
nodes.author: Hyuk Lim, Lu-Chuan Kung, Jennifer C. Hou, Haiyun Luo
nodes.norm_label: zero-configuration robust indoor localization - svd-based system (lim et al., uiuc tr 2005)

nodes.label: Truncated SVD for Online RSS-to-Distance Mapping Without Site Survey
nodes.source_file: raw/Zero-Configuration, Robust Indoor Localization Theory and Experimentation.pdf
nodes.rationale: Using truncated SVD on online AP-to-AP RSS measurements creates a real-time calibration of the RF environment without site surveys, capturing dynamic effects (door states, furniture, people) and bootstrapping zero-configuration localization.
nodes.norm_label: truncated svd for online rss-to-distance mapping without site survey

nodes.label: Offline/Online Phases of RSS Fingerprinting-Based Localization
nodes.source_file: raw/Sci-Hub. Optimal placement of reference nodes for wireless indoor positioning systems : 2014 11th In.pdf
nodes.rationale: The two-phase structure (offline survey + online matching) is universal to fingerprinting systems; offline builds a radio map by RSS site survey, online matches live RSS to the map. This design separates calibration cost from operational cost.
nodes.norm_label: offline/online phases of rss fingerprinting-based localization

nodes.label: Low-Rank Matrix Completion for Radio Map Construction
nodes.source_file: raw/Wang et al. - 2021 - Fast Construction of the Radio Map Based on the Im.pdf | My Library | Zotero.pdf
nodes.rationale: RSS data exhibits spatial correlation and low-rank structure; exploiting this via matrix completion fills a sparse set of measured reference points into a complete radio map, reducing manual data collection cost.
nodes.norm_label: low-rank matrix completion for radio map construction

nodes.label: Distribution of WLAN RSSI for Indoor Location Determination
nodes.author: Kamol Kaemarungsi
nodes.norm_label: distribution of wlan rssi for indoor location determination

nodes.label: RSSI Distribution Properties (Left-Skew, Lognormal)
nodes.author: Kamol Kaemarungsi
nodes.rationale: Understanding the actual statistical distribution of RSSI (left-skewed at strong signal, approximately lognormal at weak signal) is key to improving indoor fingerprinting positioning performance and building better radio maps instead of storing entire histograms.

nodes.label: WLAN Card Hardware Variation in RSSI Reporting
nodes.author: Kamol Kaemarungsi
nodes.rationale: Different WLAN chipsets implement RF measurement differently (range, granularity, mapping), requiring consistent card use during both offline and online phases of fingerprinting to avoid cross-card RSSI mismatch errors.
nodes.norm_label: wlan card hardware variation in rssi reporting

nodes.label: RADAR: In-Building RF-Based User Location and Tracking System
nodes.author: Paramvir Bahl and Venkata N. Padmanabhan
nodes.norm_label: radar: in-building rf-based user location and tracking system

nodes.label: Wall Attenuation Factor (WAF) Propagation Model
nodes.author: Paramvir Bahl and Venkata N. Padmanabhan
nodes.rationale: WAF was chosen over Rayleigh and Rician models as a compromise between simplicity and accuracy; it accounts for large-scale path loss and wall obstructions without requiring complex per-component isolation, and enables cost-effective deployment without dense empirical measurements.

nodes.label: Empirical Signal Strength Method (Offline Phase Survey)
nodes.author: Paramvir Bahl and Venkata N. Padmanabhan
nodes.norm_label: empirical signal strength method (offline phase survey)

nodes.label: User Body Orientation Effect on RSSI
nodes.author: Paramvir Bahl and Venkata N. Padmanabhan
nodes.norm_label: user body orientation effect on rssi

nodes.label: K-Nearest Neighbor Averaging in Signal Space
nodes.author: Paramvir Bahl and Venkata N. Padmanabhan
nodes.norm_label: k-nearest neighbor averaging in signal space

nodes.label: WaveLAN NIC (Lucent RF Hardware)
nodes.author: Paramvir Bahl and Venkata N. Padmanabhan

nodes.label: Indoor Multipath Propagation Effects
nodes.author: Paramvir Bahl and Venkata N. Padmanabhan

nodes.label: Floor Estimation Algorithm for Wireless Indoor Multi-Story Positioning
nodes.author: Kriangkrai Maneerat, Chutima Prommak, Kamol Kaemarungsi
nodes.norm_label: floor estimation algorithm for wireless indoor multi-story positioning

nodes.label: Sum-RSS Floor Estimation Algorithm
nodes.author: Kriangkrai Maneerat, Chutima Prommak, Kamol Kaemarungsi
nodes.rationale: Summing average RSS from all reference nodes on each floor avoids time-consuming offline radio map construction; the floor with the highest RSS sum is selected. This low-complexity approach is suited for resource-constrained WSN nodes while achieving >90% floor accuracy.

nodes.label: Nearest Floor Algorithm (KNN-based Floor Determination)
nodes.author: Kriangkrai Maneerat, Chutima Prommak, Kamol Kaemarungsi

nodes.label: Group Variance Algorithm (Floor Determination)
nodes.author: Kriangkrai Maneerat, Chutima Prommak, Kamol Kaemarungsi

nodes.label: IEEE 802.15.4 Wireless Sensor Network
nodes.author: Kriangkrai Maneerat, Chutima Prommak, Kamol Kaemarungsi

nodes.label: Multi-Floor Indoor Positioning Problem
nodes.author: Kriangkrai Maneerat, Chutima Prommak, Kamol Kaemarungsi

nodes.label: Freescale MC13224V Wireless Sensor Node
nodes.author: Kriangkrai Maneerat, Chutima Prommak, Kamol Kaemarungsi

nodes.label: Multipath Mitigation for RSSI-Based Bluetooth Low Energy Localization
nodes.author: Juthatip Wisanmongkol, Ladawan Klinkusoom, Taweesak Sanpechuda, La-or Kovavisaruch, Kamol Kaemarungsi
nodes.norm_label: multipath mitigation for rssi-based bluetooth low energy localization

nodes.label: Weighted Average Filter with Threshold (RSSI Filtering)
nodes.author: Juthatip Wisanmongkol et al.
nodes.rationale: A low-complexity filter that excludes spike outliers using a counter-threshold mechanism, updating the weighted average only when persistent change is detected. This preserves tracking ability while rejecting transient multipath spikes.
nodes.norm_label: weighted average filter with threshold (rssi filtering)

nodes.label: Maximum Ratio Combining (MRC) for BLE RSSI
nodes.author: Juthatip Wisanmongkol et al.
nodes.rationale: MRC weights contributions from multiple BLE tags proportionally to their signal strength relative to the minimum receivable level, providing more stable combined RSSI than simple mean or max combiners for space diversity.
nodes.norm_label: maximum ratio combining (mrc) for ble rssi

nodes.label: Space Diversity with Multiple BLE Tags
nodes.author: Juthatip Wisanmongkol et al.
nodes.norm_label: space diversity with multiple ble tags

nodes.label: Active Badge Infrared Location System
nodes.author: Paramvir Bahl and Venkata N. Padmanabhan

nodes.label: Cohen-Sutherland Line-Clipping Algorithm (Wall Detection)
nodes.author: Paramvir Bahl and Venkata N. Padmanabhan

nodes.label: Offline and Online Phases of Fingerprinting
nodes.author: Kamol Kaemarungsi
nodes.norm_label: offline and online phases of fingerprinting

nodes.label: 10th-90th Percentile Outlier Removal
nodes.author: Kriangkrai Maneerat, Chutima Prommak, Kamol Kaemarungsi

nodes.label: Sliding Time Window for Mobile User Tracking
nodes.author: Paramvir Bahl and Venkata N. Padmanabhan
nodes.norm_label: sliding time window for mobile user tracking

nodes.author: Wu, K.; Xiao, J.; Yi, Y.; Chen, D.; Luo, X.; Ni, L.M.

nodes.author: Sobehy, A.; Renault, É.; Mühlethaler, P.
